heading: Congressional declaration for utilization of programs under other Federal laws in fields of health and social services
label: 4542 Congressional declaration for utilization of programs under other Federal laws in fields of health and social services
text: The Congress declares that, in addition to the programs under the Comprehensive Alcohol Abuse and Alcoholism Prevention, Treatment, and Rehabilitation Act of 1970 [ et seq.], programs under other Federal laws which provide Federal or federally assisted research, prevention, treatment, or rehabilitation in the fields of health and social services should be appropriately utilized to help eradicate alcohol abuse and alcoholism as a major problem.42 U.S.C. 4541
notes: References in TextThe Comprehensive Alcohol Abuse and Alcoholism Prevention, Treatment, and Rehabilitation Act of 1970, referred to in text, is , , , which is classified principally to this chapter (§ 4541 et seq.). For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title note set out under and Tables.Pub. L. 91–61684 Stat. 1848section 4541 of this titleDec. 31, 1970
CodificationSection was not enacted as part of the Comprehensive Alcohol Abuse and Alcoholism Prevention, Treatment, and Rehabilitation Act of 1970 which comprises this chapter.
